content:
# Final Review Proposal
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.4-xhigh

## Summary

Reviewed the branch diff against `origin/master` while excluding `.ralph-burning/` from scope. The new fixture module and mock-dispatch support compile, and targeted `cargo test test_support:: -- --nocapture` passed. I found one correctness issue in how the fixture keeps mock state in sync with `.beads/issues.jsonl`, plus one acceptance-coverage gap in the smoke test’s performance assertion. `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` currently fails on unrelated existing code in `tests/unit/process_backend_test.rs:2872`, not in the new fixture changes.

## Amendments

1. `finding_summary`: `src/test_support/e2e_fixtures.rs:451-563`, `src/test_support/e2e_fixtures.rs:591-610`, and `src/test_support/e2e_fixtures.rs:671-677` make `ScenarioState.issues` the authoritative bead graph for `br list/show` and `bv --robot-next`. The mock adapters never reread `.beads/issues.jsonl`, so any code under test that rewrites the export directly will leave the filesystem and mock responses out of sync. That is a real risk in this codebase because reconciliation paths already rewrite `issues.jsonl` directly (for example `src/contexts/automation_runtime/failure_reconciliation.rs:1621-1624` and `src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:2059-2071`). This breaks the fixture’s stated contract that mocked responses stay consistent with workspace mutations. `affected_files`: [`src/test_support/e2e_fixtures.rs`]. `remediation_hint`: make `.beads/issues.jsonl` the source of truth for read commands, or refresh `ScenarioState` from disk before answering `list/show/robot-next`.
   Rationale: This fixture is intended for integration-style tests. Returning stale `br`/`bv` answers after the workspace export changes can hide real bugs or create false failures in exactly the scenarios the fixture is meant to exercise.
2. `finding_summary`: the smoke test does not enforce the bead’s performance requirement. `src/test_support/e2e_fixtures.rs:917-924` only asserts fixture creation stays under 10 seconds even though the acceptance criteria require `<1 second`. A regression into the 2-9 second range would still pass CI while violating the bead contract. `affected_files`: [`src/test_support/e2e_fixtures.rs`]. `remediation_hint`: tighten the assertion to a sub-second bound with a little headroom, so the required fast-path behavior is actually protected by the new smoke test.
   Rationale: The current implementation happens to be fast, but the only automated guard is an order of magnitude looser than the explicit requirement, so the requested performance guarantee is not actually covered.
